jokes.text: Putting away groceries, it's like unwrapping presents from yourself. Fruit Roll-Ups for Bart. Beer Roll-Ups for Homer.
jokes.setup: The relatable observation about grocery shopping feeling like getting presents
jokes.punchline: Beer Roll-Ups for Homer - absurd product that parallels Fruit Roll-Ups
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel structure with an absurd twist that perfectly captures Homer's character through an impossible product

jokes.text: Burly? I bought the wrong brand, Maggie.
jokes.setup: Marge discovers she bought the wrong paper towel brand
jokes.punchline: Addressing her concern to baby Maggie as if she'd understand or care
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Marge's domestic concerns, mild humor from talking to baby about mundane issues

jokes.text: Look at those massive plaid shoulders! Look at that absorbativity!
jokes.setup: Marge discovers the Burly paper towels
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'absorbativity' delivered with genuine enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Great made-up word that sounds technical but silly, perfectly captures Marge's excitement about household products

jokes.text: I came home as quick as I could. What's going on? Watch what happens when I spill this blue liquid.
jokes.setup: Homer rushes home for Marge's urgent call
jokes.punchline: The 'emergency' is a paper towel demonstration with mysterious blue liquid
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff showing Marge's priorities and Homer's willingness to participate in domestic drama

jokes.text: You pulled me out of school for this? Absolutely. You're about to get a lesson in value.
jokes.setup: Bart's reasonable complaint about missing school
jokes.punchline: Marge's earnest response that paper towel demonstrations are educational
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - earnestly treating trivial things as important life lessons

jokes.text: Ooh, Burly, you're insatiable.
jokes.setup: Marge testing the paper towel's absorption
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently sensual language directed at paper towel mascot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre that works on multiple levels - Marge's innocent enthusiasm takes on unintended meaning

jokes.text: Oh, Burly, you're so rugged and manly.
jokes.setup: Following the previous innuendo
jokes.punchline: Marge continues describing paper towel mascot in romantic terms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke, building the romantic subtext

jokes.text: Marge, a bee almost stung me today. I felt the wind go right by my ear.
jokes.setup: Homer interrupts with what sounds like a dramatic near-death experience
jokes.punchline: The 'danger' was feeling wind from a bee that didn't even touch him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cowardice - dramatizing the most minor non-event as if it were life-threatening

jokes.text: Fantasize, Marge. Fantasize about Burly.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort Marge while she's absorbed with paper towels
jokes.punchline: Homer encouraging his wife to have romantic fantasies about a paper towel mascot
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully uncomfortable moment where Homer's obliviousness leads him to encourage his wife's product obsession

jokes.text: Ooh! Mama Celeste. You touch me and I cut you.
jokes.setup: Homer's fantasy about a food mascot
jokes.punchline: Mama Celeste pizza mascot being aggressive and threatening
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - instead of romantic fantasy, Homer imagines a violent Italian grandmother, perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Homer, I'll tell you what I told Redford. It ain't gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Continuing the Mama Celeste fantasy
jokes.punchline: The mascot claiming to have rejected Robert Redford
jokes.explanation: Great escalation adding celebrity name-drop to the absurd scenario

jokes.text: Marge hasn't asked me for a signed photo in months.
jokes.setup: Homer reading Marge's letter to Burly
jokes.punchline: Homer's assumption that spouses regularly request signed photos of each other
jokes.explanation: Good insight into Homer's misunderstanding of normal relationships

jokes.text: Hello. This is Chad Sexington, the model for Burly Paper Towels.
jokes.setup: Homer impersonating the Burly model
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous fake name 'Chad Sexington'
jokes.explanation: Instant classic name that's become a cultural reference - perfectly over-the-top masculine name

jokes.text: I was having a private conversation with my wife in the guise of Chad Sexington. Do you mind?
jokes.setup: Bart catching Homer on the phone
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant defense of his deception using the ridiculous name matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good callback that doubles down on the absurd name while showing Homer's commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Tonight? Well, you better get your hopes up.
jokes.setup: Standard phrase would be 'don't get your hopes up'
jokes.punchline: Homer says the opposite, encouraging false hope
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that subverts the expected phrase while revealing Homer's poor planning

jokes.text: Why, look. It's Chad Sexington! Hey, baby! I'm that guy you like.
jokes.setup: Barney arriving as Homer's stand-in
jokes.punchline: Barney's terrible attempt at being suave combined with vague description
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Barney's characteristically poor execution of Homer's plan

jokes.text: Congratulations. I feel ridiculous.
jokes.setup: The failed deception being exposed
jokes.punchline: Barney's matter-of-fact assessment of his own embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan response to the awkward situation

jokes.text: It was hard on me, too. I had to wear a suit.
jokes.setup: Homer apologizing for the humiliating scheme
jokes.punchline: Barney focusing on wearing formal clothes as his main hardship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - missing the emotional point and focusing on minor physical discomfort

jokes.text: How about Benihana, where dinner is the show?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make it up to Marge with dinner and a show
jokes.punchline: Suggesting a place that combines both into one thing, missing the point
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer logic - finding the most efficient/lazy solution to a romantic gesture

jokes.text: I like the ones where nothing catches on fire. Yeah. Nothing is hurt except feelings.
jokes.setup: Discussing pranks in general
jokes.punchline: Lisa's preference for emotionally damaging but physically safe pranks
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals Lisa's sophisticated understanding of emotional vs physical harm

jokes.text: Well, what's the matter with you, champ? You couldn't find a date?
jokes.setup: Hypnotist looking for volunteers
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting Homer about being alone
jokes.explanation: Basic insult humor, sets up the hypnotist as abrasive character

jokes.text: Glayvin! That's a powerful... Whoa! Hold it 'cause it's different!
jokes.setup: Professor Frink being hypnotized
jokes.punchline: His characteristic scientific babbling while under hypnosis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frink's personality bleeding through hypnosis

jokes.text: Hey, cupcake, listen good. I want you to swallow that gum and meet me in the coatroom, in five, four, three, two, now.
jokes.setup: Frink hypnotized to be a make-out artist
jokes.punchline: Nerdy professor awkwardly trying to be smooth with specific instructions
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Frink's awkward personality and the suave character he's supposed to be

jokes.text: Oh, dear. I've redorkulated.
jokes.setup: Frink coming out of hypnosis
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining 'dork' with scientific terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant made-up word that perfectly captures both Frink's scientific speech pattern and self-awareness

jokes.text: Hey, smart move, Four-Eyes. Sitting next to Skeletor here makes you look like Hercules.
jokes.setup: Hypnotist continuing to insult audience members
jokes.punchline: Comparing thin man to cartoon villain while backhanded-complimenting another
jokes.explanation: Establishes the hypnotist's mean-spirited character, though relies on appearance-based humor

jokes.text: Actually, my father died a long time ago.
jokes.setup: Hypnotist's joke about taking father out before he dies
jokes.punchline: Earnest correction that deflates the joke awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment that deflates the hypnotist's momentum

jokes.text: Okay. Is anyone here not a downer? Anyone?
jokes.setup: Hypnotist's act falling apart due to depressing responses
jokes.punchline: His desperate plea for someone upbeat
jokes.explanation: Shows the hypnotist's act completely derailing, setting up Homer's entrance

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm a famous historian! Out of my way!
jokes.setup: Homer hypnotized to be a famous historian
jokes.punchline: Treating being a historian like it makes you physically dominant
jokes.explanation: Great absurd take on academic achievement as source of physical confidence

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm Angie Dickinson! Out of my way!
jokes.setup: Supposed to be hypnotized as Emily Dickinson
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing the poet with actress Angie Dickinson
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer mistake - confusing intellectual figure with pop culture reference he actually knows

jokes.text: I'm 12 years old. I'm with my friends. It's a beautiful summer day at the old swimming hole. Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Peaceful childhood memory being described
jokes.punchline: Sudden traumatic reaction suggesting something horrible
jokes.explanation: Good tonal shift from peaceful to horrific, sets up the episode's main plot

jokes.text: Yeah. He ruined naptime and quiet time.
jokes.setup: Homer being disruptive at work
jokes.punchline: Adult workplace having naptime and quiet time like a daycare
jokes.explanation: Good joke about the nuclear plant's relaxed work environment

jokes.text: Oh, cool. He's still mental.
jokes.setup: Homer showing signs of trauma
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual, callous observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart insensitivity to family drama

jokes.text: It would be a good excuse to use my Yaqui tea set.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Native American memory tea
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical concern about using matching dinnerware
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment showing her precocious knowledge paired with normal kid priorities

jokes.text: I remember that time I tried to jump over Springfield Gorge. I'm gonna make it!
jokes.setup: Homer recalling memories
jokes.punchline: Reference to famous scene where he obviously didn't make it
jokes.explanation: Callback to famous earlier episode moment

jokes.text: No, Dad. Everyone's sick of that memory.
jokes.setup: Homer bringing up the gorge incident again
jokes.punchline: Lisa speaking for the audience's fatigue with repeated references
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke acknowledging audience fatigue with overused references

jokes.text: Check it out, Fat Tony. Those jokers think they're The Cowsills.
jokes.setup: Kids singing harmonies
jokes.punchline: Mafia connecting innocent singing to 1960s family singing group
jokes.explanation: Decent period reference that shows mobsters' pop culture awareness

jokes.text: You guys have blundered into our secret tobaccy patch. Wow! Is that wacky tobaccy?
jokes.setup: Kids discovering illegal crop
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent misunderstanding of drug terminology
jokes.explanation: Shows young Homer's naivety about drugs through innocent wordplay

jokes.text: Eighteen more pumps, that could break the skin.
jokes.setup: Moe threatening with BB gun
jokes.punchline: Mobster's genuine concern about the minimal threat
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between dangerous criminals and pathetic weapon threat

jokes.text: Look at all them stars. Bunch of lazy lights, don't do nothing for nobody.
jokes.setup: Romantic stargazing moment
jokes.punchline: Moe's aggressively negative take on natural beauty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - turning beauty into something to complain about with unique phrasing

jokes.text: Have you heard about this Internet thing? Yeah. It's the inner netting they invented to line swim trunks.
jokes.setup: 1970s characters discussing the future Internet
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding based on literal interpretation of name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anachronistic joke that makes perfect logical sense from 1970s perspective

jokes.text: We're the Moe Szyslak Experience, featuring Homer.
jokes.setup: Friends debating their group name
jokes.punchline: Moe's grandiose name that reduces Homer to featured player
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing young Moe's ego and delusions of grandeur

jokes.text: Not me. I'm shaking like a French soldier.
jokes.setup: Being scared to jump from high place
jokes.punchline: Dated stereotype about French military courage
jokes.explanation: Relies on outdated stereotype, period-appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Yeah. I think I just logged on to my Internet.
jokes.setup: Continuing the swim trunk Internet misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Using modern Internet terminology for getting in swimming trunks
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the original joke with modern terminology

jokes.text: Fine. Crap all over my theory.
jokes.setup: Homer disagreeing with Moe's explanation
jokes.punchline: Moe's overly dramatic response to mild disagreement
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe defensiveness and self-pity

jokes.text: Come on, Homer! We're going to Sears to feel the bras!
jokes.setup: Kids leaving the scene
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate but innocent childhood activity
jokes.explanation: Captures awkward prepubescent curiosity in specific, memorable way

jokes.text: My occasional overeating, my fear of corpses.
jokes.setup: Homer blaming his problems on childhood trauma
jokes.punchline: Describing massive overeating as 'occasional' and having an unusual specific phobia
jokes.explanation: Good understatement joke paired with oddly specific phobia

jokes.text: Hey, you guys can come with us. No, no, no. He said 'Simpson family.'
jokes.setup: Homer inviting friends along
jokes.punchline: Moe and others taking exclusion very literally and seriously
jokes.explanation: Awkward social moment showing characters' sensitivity to exclusion

jokes.text: Yep. The old quarry is just a stone's throw away. Stop saying that, Dad. Never.
jokes.setup: Homer using quarry-related pun
jokes.punchline: Bart's exasperation and Homer's stubborn commitment to bad wordplay
jokes.explanation: Homer's commitment to Dad jokes despite family annoyance

jokes.text: Seventeen seventy-six. How much is left in my checking account.
jokes.setup: Hypnotist appearing randomly
jokes.punchline: Using his powers for mundane financial information
jokes.explanation: Deflating use of mystical powers for everyday concerns

jokes.text: Someone's yellow belly is showing. Oh, sorry.
jokes.setup: Wiggum calling someone cowardly
jokes.punchline: Homer's shirt literally showing his yellow belly
jokes.explanation: Visual pun that works on both literal and figurative levels

jokes.text: And maybe the city would give me a coupon for free guitar lessons.
jokes.setup: Wiggum wanting to be a hero
jokes.punchline: His modest, oddly specific reward expectation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum moment showing his humble, specific aspirations

jokes.text: Put it back. I don't want to see it this way.
jokes.setup: Finding empty shopping cart instead of body
jokes.punchline: Homer's emotional attachment to seeing cart in its natural habitat
jokes.explanation: Homer's unexpected sentimentality about a shopping cart

jokes.text: Burly to the rescue!
jokes.setup: Need to drain water to find body
jokes.punchline: Marge using her obsession with paper towels practically
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes the earlier setup pay off practically

jokes.text: Don't get excited. It's just a skull-shaped rock and a bunch of white sticks.
jokes.setup: Finding suspicious objects
jokes.punchline: Lisa's deadpan description of obvious human remains
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa deadpan delivery describing obvious skeleton euphemistically

jokes.text: And someone has eaten the flesh.
jokes.setup: Examining the skeleton
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact assessment of decomposition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Lisa's characteristic clinical detachment

jokes.text: Now do you believe dead rats float, Lisa? Yes.
jokes.setup: Serious murder investigation
jokes.punchline: Homer using moment to prove trivial point about rat buoyancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - using serious situation to settle petty argument

jokes.text: Did I say corpse hatch? I meant 'innocence tube.'
jokes.setup: Burns accidentally revealing corpse disposal method
jokes.punchline: Transparently obvious cover-up with innocent-sounding name
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns moment - terrible cover-up that makes situation worse

jokes.text: Ooh! A movie! I call the couch!
jokes.setup: About to watch evidence of murder
jokes.punchline: Homer's excitement about getting comfortable for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Homer missing the gravity of situation and treating murder evidence like entertainment

jokes.text: So, you're a baby, huh? How's that working out for you?
jokes.setup: Burns talking to baby Smithers in flashback
jokes.punchline: Addressing infant with adult conversation starter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns social awkwardness - treating baby like networking contact

jokes.text: I told him his father was killed in the Amazon by a tribe of savage women.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining his lie about Smithers' father
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and elaborate fake death story
jokes.explanation: Typical Burns elaborate deception with oddly specific details

jokes.text: Why? He'd just bury it.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to give skull to Smithers
jokes.punchline: Homer's logical but callous reasoning about normal burial practices
jokes.explanation: Homer's practical but insensitive logic about what people do with human remains

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Oh, yeah. I sleuthed my ass off on this one.
jokes.setup: Moe presenting his detective work
jokes.punchline: Crude but enthusiastic description of his investigative effort
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe enthusiasm with characteristically crude language

jokes.text: This is a shell that, to me, this is just me talking. It looks like a helmet for a mouse.
jokes.setup: Moe presenting evidence
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical observation delivered with false authority
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd observation that perfectly captures Moe's delusional detective work

jokes.text: But if you ask the mice about it, they don't say nothing. I mean, they run the other way.
jokes.setup: Mouse helmet theory
jokes.punchline: Interpreting normal mouse behavior as suspicious conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that doubles down on the absurd mouse conspiracy theory

jokes.text: You really made that envelope? 'Cause it says 'Hammermill' over here. Um... No.
jokes.setup: Moe claiming he made envelope for his evidence
jokes.punchline: Lisa catching him in obvious lie about manufacturer label
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Moe's pretensions caught by Lisa's attention to detail
